entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: The legume crop being studied for salinity tolerance.

entities.name: SALINITY TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to maintain growth and productivity under high salt conditions.

entities.name: IMAGE-BASED PHENOTYPING
entities.description: A high-throughput technique used to measure plant traits using images.

entities.name: SOIL SALINITY
entities.description: The presence of excess salts in the soil that reduces crop productivity.

entities.name: PLANT GROWTH RATE
entities.description: The rate at which a plant increases in size, measured by tracking leaf expansion.

entities.name: PLANT HEIGHT
entities.description: The vertical measurement of the plant from the base to the tip.

entities.name: SHOOT BIOMASS
entities.description: The total mass of the above-ground parts of the plant.

entities.name: POD ABORTION
entities.description: The premature shedding of pods due to stress.

entities.name: POD FILLING
entities.description: The process of seed development and accumulation of nutrients within the pod.

entities.name: SEED NUMBER
entities.description: The total count of seeds produced per plant, identified as a major determinant for salinity tolerance.

entities.name: SEED YIELD
entities.description: The total weight or quantity of seeds harvested.

entities.name: GENETIC VARIATION
entities.description: The diversity in genetic makeup among different accessions of a species.

entities.name: GLASSHOUSE
entities.description: A controlled environment used for conducting plant assays.

entities.name: FIELD SITES
entities.description: Natural outdoor environments used to validate glasshouse results.

entities.name: SHOOT ION INDEPENDENT STRESS
entities.description: Stress in the shoot resulting from hydraulic resistance and osmotic stress rather than ion toxicity.

entities.name: HYDRAULIC RESISTANCE
entities.description: The resistance to the flow of water through the plant's vascular system.

entities.name: OSMOTIC STRESS
entities.description: Stress caused by the imbalance of water potential between the soil and the plant roots.
